title: Model Context Protocol reference servers
text: The official reference collection documents copyable MCP client configs: TypeScript servers run directly with `npx`, Python servers with `uvx`, and clients like Claude Desktop can register filesystem, git, GitHub, or Postgres servers with explicit command/args blocks.

title: Filesystem MCP server
text: The filesystem server exposes safe read/write/search tools, requires at least one allowed directory, and recommends Roots-capable clients so allowed directories can update at runtime instead of forcing a server restart for every path change.

title: Filesystem MCP server
text: VS Code can install the filesystem server from user `mcp.json` or workspace `.vscode/mcp.json`, while Claude Desktop can run the same server with `npx` or Docker and mount only the directories the agent should see.

title: MCP servers and NVM path gotchas
text: Real users reported that `npx`-based MCP servers can fail under NVM-managed Node installs, and shared working fixes based on absolute `node` paths, wrapper scripts, or explicit PATH/NODE_PATH config in the MCP server entry.

title: HN discussion on MCP boundaries
text: The discussion repeatedly frames MCP as a security and policy boundary: deterministic gates, proxied credentials, and explicit tool interfaces are the reasons teams adopt MCP instead of letting agents loose on broader shell access.

title: Claude Code GitHub Actions
text: Anthropic's docs show both quick setup with `/install-github-app` and manual setup with the Claude GitHub App, `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, and a workflow file. The same page includes comment-triggered, skills-based, and cron-driven automation examples.

title: Claude Code GitHub Actions
text: The v1 docs highlight auto-detected modes, `CLAUDE.md` support, `prompt`, and `claude_args`, making repo-native automation cleaner than hand-rolled bot glue while keeping execution on GitHub-hosted runners.

title: Custom subagents in scheduled Claude workflows
text: A maintainer discussion shows the practical pattern for scheduled custom agents: store the agent in `.claude/agents/`, invoke it with `claude_args: --agent fixer`, and use `--add-dir` when one workflow needs access to more than one checked-out repo.

title: Codex quickstart
text: OpenAI's quickstart walks through install, sign-in, project selection, and local agent mode, and explicitly recommends Git checkpoints before and after tasks so teams can adopt the tool without losing rollback discipline.

title: Codex CLI
text: The CLI page describes Codex as open source and built in Rust for speed and efficiency, and it bundles local code review, subagents, web search, MCP, scripting, and cloud tasks into one terminal workflow.

title: OpenAI Evals build guide
text: The build guide turns eval work into a repeatable guardrail: dataset in JSONL, YAML registration, local `oaieval` runs, and model-graded meta-evals with labeled choices so `metascore/` accuracy stays close to `1.0` before teams trust the rubric.

title: OpenAI Evals CI gate
text: The repository's workflow watches new YAML files under `evals/registry/**`, installs the package, and runs `oaieval dummy $first_key --max_samples 10` on pull requests so new eval definitions are exercised before merge.

title: AI Gateway provider sorting
text: Vercel documents `providerOptions.gateway.sort` for `cost`, `ttft`, and `tps`, plus metadata showing execution order, per-provider metrics, and deprioritized providers. This makes provider choice inspectable instead of hidden inside the gateway.

title: AI Gateway sort use cases
text: The changelog clarifies when each sort matters: `cost` for high-volume work, `ttft` for interactive latency, and `tps` for long outputs. Ranking updates at request time as providers or prices change, so teams do not hardcode one static ordering.

title: AI Gateway model fallbacks
text: The fallback docs show how one request can try a primary model, then backup models, while also honoring provider preference for each model. The response metadata exposes every `modelAttempt` and `providerAttempt`, including failures.

title: AI Gateway reliability architecture
text: Vercel's architecture write-up says AI Gateway reroutes traffic when a provider region slows down and automatically retries with the next provider or even another model. The post also quantifies the system's cost profile: less than 8% of runtime was active CPU work in its first month.
